summary: Iter 21 — Phase D1 ESP32 hardware bridge + Phase A/B/C HUD endpoint live-data audit + AtlasSidePanel cleanup. 24/24 backend pytest GREEN (test_iter21_esp32_safety.py: 18 HUD endpoints + MQTT dormant + 6 ESP32 lifecycle/safety). Frontend smoke GREEN — all 15 ring tiles render (AJANI/MINERVA/HERMES/COUNCIL + MANUAL/CYCLOPEDIA/MEMORY/SYSTEMS/CUSTOMIZATION + SUBJECTS/LAB/PROJECTS/BLUEPRINTS/ARCHIVE/EXPLORE MODE), zero references to forbidden `worlds`/`hyperaxel`, atlas-sidepanel-demo-badge removed, BLUEPRINTS tile opens live BlueprintWorkbench. Both critical safety bugs (sticky safe_state + emergency_stop inbox enqueueing) are now verified end-to-end with the python sim.
critical_code_review_comments: Robot safety contract is now correct & defended end-to-end: (a) services/robot.emergency_stop now enqueues a real `emergency_stop` command into the device inbox in addition to flipping status=safe_state — the python sim confirms it picks the command up within ~4s and prints `EMERGENCY STOP — safe state`; (b) services/robot.ingest_telemetry no longer unconditionally promotes status=online — if the device is in safe_state, telemetry is accepted but status stays sticky. Verified: after emergency-stop the sim kept POSTing telemetry for ~3 s and device GET still returned status=safe_state. Subsequent motion command came back with status=rejected + rejection_reason containing SAFE_STATE.

MQTT uplink subscriber is correctly dormant when MQTT_BROKER_HOST is unset — /api/robot/mqtt/status returns {enabled:false, connected:false, topic_prefix:'atlas', last_error:null} and no exceptions are logged on startup.

AtlasSidePanel.js is clean — `opData` dead dict is gone, atlas-sidepanel-demo-badge testid is gone, every routed op (blueprints/subjects/lab/systems/manual/encyclopedia/memory/customization/archive/explore/projects) opens a real backend-wired workbench, and any unrouted op falls through to the explicit atlas-sidepanel-not-implemented red block. Good architectural discipline — refuses to render fake content.
ringStructure.js contains exactly the 4 inner + 5 middle + 6 outer items requested. No `worlds` or `hyperaxel` ids anywhere in /app/frontend/src.

Response shapes are inconsistent across the HUD endpoints — some use `items`, some use `entries` (archive), some use `lessons` vs `items`, some return a bare list (`/api/persona/list`). All work today, but a future contract-test pass that normalises every list endpoint to `{items: [...], count: N}` would prevent silent breakage when collection keys are renamed. The first run of the iter21 test caught 4 such mismatches before being relaxed to accept multiple key spellings.

ESP32 simulator (/app/hardware/esp32/sim/atlas_node_sim.py) is a faithful port of the Arduino loop — same command kinds, same safe_state precedence (ignored commands when in safe_state), same JSON payload schema. Drop-in usable for CI.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iter21_esp32_safety.py (NEW — 24 tests: 18 HUD endpoint live-data + MQTT dormant + 6 ESP32 lifecycle/safety)

success_rate.frontend: 100% smoke — 15/15 ring tiles, 0 forbidden tokens, demo-badge removed, BLUEPRINTS opens live workbench

test_credentials: All robot ownership calls require header X-Atlas-Role: owner — already wired in conftest-free test file.
seed_data_creation: test_iter21_esp32_safety.py registered one TEST_ESP32_iter21 device (auto-deleted via module-scoped teardown), pushed ~10 http_sim telemetry frames, and exercised actuate/emergency_stop/motion(rejected)/clear_safe_state on it. No persistent test data left behind.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase D1 ESP32 stack is fully verified — backend + python sim + safety contract all GREEN. The two latent bugs (sticky safe_state on telemetry ingest, emergency_stop not enqueueing) are now defended by automated tests. MQTT uplink remains dormant by design (MQTT_BROKER_HOST unset). The Arduino firmware (/app/hardware/esp32/atlas_node/atlas_node.ino) was NOT exercised — only the python sim was. If real hardware shows up, run the same lifecycle test with the device flashed and `--backend` pointed at the local backend. All 18 HUD-backing endpoints from iter21 HUD_ENDPOINTS list are LIVE on the public preview URL. Iter20 known infra limits remain: /research-orch/orchestrator/loop for cycles>=2 still 502s via Cloudflare (background-job pattern recommended); Google Patents search still 503s from cluster egress IP (downstream fallback compensates).
rca of the issue: N/A — both safety bugs from D1 were fixed by main agent before this iteration; iter21 supplies the automated regression tests so they cannot silently regress. Reproduction recipe is encoded in test_emergency_stop_enqueues_and_is_sticky.
